Wednesday, March 18, 2026 Daily Digest

Alibaba Launches Wukong, AI Maturity Gap Hits 47%, Adobe + NVIDIA Target Marketing Automation

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

Alibaba Launches Wukong: Multi-Agent AI Platform for Enterprise

Alibaba just dropped Wukong — a platform that coordinates multiple AI agents to handle document editing, spreadsheet updates, meeting transcription, and research within a single interface. Available via DingTalk (20M+ corporate users) and soon integrating with Slack, Teams, and WeChat. The "agent orchestration" wave is here.

Read on Economic Times →
2

The AI Maturity Gap: 47% Higher Margins for Leaders

New research analyzing 847 public companies shows AI-mature enterprises now achieve 47% higher operating margins than laggards — up from 21% just 18 months ago. Only 3% of enterprises have reached "transforming" stage (AI fundamental to business model), while 60% remain stuck at early stages. The compounding advantage is real.

Read on Market Research Blog →
3

Adobe + NVIDIA Partner on AI Marketing Assets

Adobe's Firefly plus NVIDIA's Omniverse-powered digital twins could replace dozens of photoshoots with AI-rendered pack shots and scenes that update automatically when packaging or pricing changes. For marketing teams, this is the death of the traditional product shoot workflow.

Read on TechGenyz →
4

Gartner: 40% of Enterprise Apps Will Include AI Agents by 2026

According to Salsify's new B2B ecommerce report, Gartner now estimates 40% of enterprise applications will embed AI agents this year. The shift: from standalone AI tools to AI woven into existing workflows. This means AI capabilities arriving inside the tools you already use.

Read on Salsify →
5

Product Management in 2026: The AI PM Roadmap

Medium piece breaking down five major changes redefining Product Management — from AI-assisted user research to automated roadmap prioritization. The parallel to marketing is clear: both functions are seeing AI absorb the "gathering and synthesizing" work, pushing humans toward strategy and judgment calls.

Read on Medium →

💡 My Take

Read this one: The AI Maturity Gap research. That 47% margin advantage — widened from 21% in just 18 months — is the clearest signal yet that AI adoption isn't optional. The gap is compounding. If your org is still "exploring," you're not behind — you're falling further behind every quarter.
